revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |名称 = 白鳥神社 |画像 = [[File:IMG_shiratori1.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (しらとりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[ファイル:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> 画像:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine 画像:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) 画像:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>
== References ==
* ''{{ill|Wakamatsu Town Local History|ja|若松町郷土誌}}'' (1980 [Shōwa 55]). Wakamatsu Town Board of Education. * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Register|ja|五島神社誌}}'' (1943 [Shōwa 18], facsimile edition). Jingikai Minami-Matsuura District Office.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Shiratori Shrine}} [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>
== References ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11580594]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi-jinja (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}

Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>

According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>
== References ==
* ''{{ill|Wakamatsu Town Local History|ja|若松町郷土誌}}'' (1980 [Shōwa 55]). Wakamatsu Town Board of Education. * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Register|ja|五島神社誌}}'' (1943 [Shōwa 18], facsimile edition). Jingikai Minami-Matsuura District Office.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11580594]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>
== References ==
* ''{{ill|Wakamatsu Town Local History|ja|若松町郷土誌}}'' (1980 [Shōwa 55]). Wakamatsu Town Board of Education. * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Register|ja|五島神社誌}}'' (1943 [Shōwa 18], facsimile edition). Jingikai Minami-Matsuura District Office.

In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.

;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}

Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11580594]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>
== References ==
* ''{{ill|Wakamatsu Town Local History|ja|若松町郷土誌}}'' (1980 [Shōwa 55]). Wakamatsu Town Board of Education. * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Register|ja|五島神社誌}}'' (1943 [Shōwa 18], facsimile edition). Jingikai Minami-Matsuura District Office.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580594]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>
== References ==
* ''{{ill|Wakamatsu Town Local History|ja|若松町郷土誌}}'' (1980 [Shōwa 55]). Wakamatsu Town Board of Education. * ''{{ill|Goto Shrine Register|ja|五島神社誌}}'' (1943 [Shōwa 18], facsimile edition). Jingikai Minami-Matsuura District Office.

Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580594]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shinkamigoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>

Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

{{moved from|Shiratori Shrine (Shin‑Kami Goto Town)}} <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白鳥神社 (新上五島町)]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称 = 白鳥神社 |image = File:IMG shiratori1.JPG |所在地 = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shin-Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}間伏郷字若山19 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|53|42.6|N|128|59|49.4|E|scale:25000|name=白鳥神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 享和3年（1803年） |本殿 = |別名 = 白鳥宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月15日 |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.895167|N|128.997056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shin-Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
<gallery> File:IMG_mabushi.JPG|Mabushi Shrine File:IMG_shiogama1.JPG|Shiogama Shrine (Sakanoura-go) File:IMG_wakamatsu2.JPG|Wakamatsu Shrine (Wakamatsu-go) </gallery>

Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

'''Shiratori Shrine''' (白鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} located in the Wakayama area of Mabushi-go, {{ill|Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki|ja|新上五島町|1=Shin-Kamigotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206790}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|1=Nagasaki Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q169376}}.
== Enshrined deity ==
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.
== Festivals and rites ==
;Major festivals and rituals * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|1=Reisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385469}} (15 September)
{{clear}}
== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.

{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is worshipped as the principal deity.
== History ==
[[File:IMG_shiratori2.JPG|170px|thumb|Shrine buildings]] The shrine was founded in {{ill|Kyōwa era|ja|享和|1=Kyōwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q960763}} 3 (1803) as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} for the communities of Mabushi-go and Sakanoura-go. According to legend, a {{ill|Swan|ja|白鳥|1=swan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q34384}} landed on the site, prompting the establishment of the shrine.
In {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3125096}} 4 (1872) it was ranked as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on 27 April {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}} 3 (1928) it was designated a {{ill|Shrines eligible to receive offerings|ja|神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.

== Other shrines ==
Other shrines in Mabushi-go include the Mabushi district’s '''{{ill|Mabushi Shrine|ja|間伏神社|1=Mabushi Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655374}}''', the Unose district’s '''{{ill|Anagami Shrine|ja|穴神神社}}''', the Tsutsunoura district’s '''{{ill|Tsutsunoura Shrine|ja|筒ノ浦神社}}''', the Ishitsukasa district’s '''{{ill|Ishitsukasa Shrine|ja|石司神社}}''', and the Takigawara district’s '''{{ill|Takigawara Shrine|ja|瀧川原神社}}'''.
Neighbouring shrines include '''{{ill|Wakamatsu Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|若松神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Wakamatsu Shrine|lt_ja=若松神社|1=Wakamatsu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616430}}''' in Wakamatsu-go and '''{{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Shinkamigoto)|ja|塩釜神社 (新上五島町)|lt=Shiogama Shrine|lt_ja=塩釜神社|1=Shiogama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228701}}''' in Sakanoura-go.
